---
title: "Thalia Lovegrass (Eragrostis atrovirens)"
slug: "eragrostis-atrovirens"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474780354/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Thalia Lovegrass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474780354/medium.jpg" alt="Thalia Lovegrass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/acdcarroll">
acdcarroll</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264340272">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=162376)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in disturbed areas, sandy sites, and along road shoulders.

## Natural history notes

A tufted grass characterized by its fine leaves and airy, branching seed heads that often have a delicate appearance. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as marsh edges, ditches, and low-lying fields. It grows primarily during the warmer months and contributes to ground cover and vegetation structure in wetland areas.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/162376-Eragrostis-atrovirens)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/1294)
